name: English Crumpets (Bakestone Recipes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the flours and salt into a warm bowl.
2. Cream the yeast with the sugar.
3. Add the warmed water, then the oil to the yeast mixture.
4. Stir in the flour to make a batter, and beat vigorously until smooth and elastic.
5. Cover the bowl and place it in a warm place. Let it rise and become full of bubbles for about 1 1/2 hours.
6. Beat the dough with a wooden spoon until smooth.
7. Cover and let it prove again in a warm place for another 30 minutes.
8. To cook, lightly grease a bakestone.
9. Grease crumpet rings (3-3 1/2 inches) or scone cutters and place them on the bakestone to heat.
10. Cook as many crumpets as possible at a time, since the batter will not stay bubbly for long.
11. Pour about 1/2 inch of batter into each ring.
12. Cook gently for 7 - 10 minutes, or until the surface sets and is full of tiny bubbles.
13. Using an oven glove, carefully lift off the ring.
14. If the bottom of the crumpet is pale gold, flip it over and cook for another 3 minutes until lightly colored on the other side.
15. If the crumpet sticks slightly in the ring, gently push it out with the back of a wooden spoon.
16. Wipe, grease, and heat the rings for each batch of crumpets.
17. If serving immediately, wrap the crumpets in cloth and keep warm between batches.
18. Butter generously and serve at once.
19. If reheating, toast the crumpets under the grill, cooking the smooth surface first and then the top so that the butter will melt into the holes.
